sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: θεοὺς μὲν αἰτῶ τῶνδ ἀπαλλαγὴν πόνων ,
sections.translation: Release from this weary task of mine has been my plea to the gods throughout this long year ’ s watch , in which , lying upon the palace roof of the Atreidae , upon my bent arm , like a dog , I have learned to know well the gathering of the night ’ s stars , those radiant potentates conspicuous in the firmament ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: καὶ τοὺς φέροντας χεῖμα καὶ θέρος βροτοῖς
sections.translation:
bringers of winter and summer to mankind the constellations , when they rise and set .
So now I am still watching for the signal-flame , the gleaming fire that is to bring news from Troy and

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ἁλώσιμόν τε βάξιν : ὧδε γὰρ κρατεῖ
sections.translation: tidings of its capture . For thus commands my queen , woman in passionate heart and man in strength of purpose . And whenever I make here my bed , restless and dank with dew and unvisited by dreams — for instead of sleep fear stands ever by my side ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: τὸ μὴ βεβαίως βλέφαρα συμβαλεῖν ὕπνῳ :
sections.translation: so that I cannot close my eyelids fast in sleep — and whenever I care to sing or hum ( and thus apply an antidote of song to ward off drowsiness ), then my tears start forth , as I bewail the fortunes of this house of ours , not ordered for the best as in days gone by .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: νῦν δ εὐτυχὴς γένοιτ ἀπαλλαγὴ πόνων
sections.translation:
But tonight may there come a happy release from my weary task ! May the fire with its glad tidings flash through the gloom ! The signal fire suddenly flashes out
Oh welcome , you blaze in the night , a light as if of day , you harbinger of many a choral dance in Argos in thanksgiving for this glad event !

sections.label: Section 25
sections.translation:
Hallo ! Hallo !
To Agamemnon ’ s queen I thus cry aloud the signal to rise from her bed , and as quickly as she can to lift up in her palace halls a shout of joy in welcome of this fire , if the city of Ilium

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ἑάλωκεν , ὡς ὁ φρυκτὸς ἀγγέλλων [*] πρέπει :
sections.translation:
truly is taken , as this beacon unmistakably announces . And I will make an overture with a dance upon my own account ; for my lord ’ s lucky roll I shall count to my own score , now that this beacon has thrown me triple six .
Ah well , may the master of the house come home and may

sections.label: Section 35
sections.translation: I clasp his welcome hand in mine ! For the rest I stay silent ; a great ox stands upon my tongue [*] — yet the house itself , could it but speak , might tell a plain enough tale ; since , for my part , by my own choice I have words for those who know , and to those who do not know , I ’ ve lost my memory . He descends by an inner stairway ; attendants kindle fires at the altars placed in front of the palace . Enter the chorus of Argive Elders

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: δέκατον μὲν ἔτος τόδ ἐπεὶ Πριάμου [*]
sections.translation: This is now the tenth year since Priam ’ s mighty adversary , king Menelaus , and with him king Agamemnon , the mighty pair of Atreus ’ sons , joined in honor of throne and sceptre by Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 45
sections.translation:
set forth from this land with an army of a thousand ships manned by Argives , a warrior force to champion their cause .
Loud rang the battle-cry they uttered in their rage , just as eagles scream which ,

sections.label: Section 50
sections.translation: in lonely grief for their brood , rowing with the oars of their wings , wheel high over their bed , because they have lost the toil of guarding their nurslings ’ nest .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ὕπατος δ ἀίων ἤ τις Ἀπόλλων
sections.translation: But some one of the powers supreme — Apollo perhaps or Pan , or Zeus — hears the shrill wailing scream of the clamorous birds , these sojourners in his realm , and against the transgressors sends vengeance at last though late .

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: οὕτω δ Ἀτρέως παῖδας ὁ κρείσσων
sections.translation: Even so Zeus , whose power is over all , Zeus , lord of host and guest , sends against Alexander the sons of Atreus , that for the sake of a woman with many husbands [*] he may inflict many and wearying struggles ( when the knee is pressed in the dust and

sections.label: Section 65
sections.translation:
the spear is splintered in the onset ) on Danaans and on Trojans alike .
The case now stands where it stands — it moves to fulfilment at its destined end . Not by offerings burned in secret , not by secret libations ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.translation:
not by tears , shall man soften the stubborn wrath of unsanctified sacrifices . [*]
But we , incapable of service by reason of our aged frame , discarded from that martial mustering of long ago , wait here at home ,

sections.label: Section 75
sections.translation: supporting on our canes a strength like a child ’ s . For just as the vigor of youth , leaping up within the breast , is like that of old age , since the war-god is not in his place ; so extreme age , its leaves

sections.label: Section 80
sections.translation: already withering , goes its way on triple feet , and , no better than a child , wanders , a dream that is dreamed by day .

sections.label: Section 83
sections.translation: But , O daughter of Tyndareos , Queen Clytaemestra ,

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: τί χρέος ; τί νέον ; τί δ ἐπαισθομένη ,
sections.translation: what has happened ? What news do you have ? On what intelligence and convinced by what report do you send about your messengers to command sacrifice ? For all the gods our city worships , the gods supreme , the gods below ,

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: τῶν τ οὐρανίων τῶν τ ἀγοραίων ,
sections.translation: the gods of the heavens and of the marketplace , have their altars ablaze with offerings . Now here , now there , the flames rise high as heaven , yielding

sections.label: Section 95
sections.translation: to the soft and guileless persuasion of holy ointment , the sacrificial oil itself brought from the inner chambers of the palace . Of all this declare whatever you can and dare reveal , and be a healer of my uneasy heart .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: ἣ νῦν τοτὲ μὲν κακόφρων τελέθει ,
sections.translation: This now at one moment bodes ill , while then again hope , shining with kindly light from the sacrifices , wards off the biting care of the sorrow that gnaws my heart .

sections.label: Section 1.104
sections.original: κύριός εἰμι θροεῖν ὅδιον κράτος αἴσιον ἀνδρῶν
sections.translation: I have the power to proclaim the augury of triumph given on their way

sections.label: Section 1.105
sections.translation: to princely men — since my age [*] still breathes Persuasion upon me from the gods , the strength of song — how the twin-throned command of the Achaeans ,

sections.label: Section 1.110
sections.translation: the single-minded captains of Hellas ’ youth , with avenging spear and arm against the Teucrian land , was sent off by the inspiring omen appearing to the kings of the ships — kingly birds ,

sections.label: Section 1.115
sections.original: ῶν ὁ κελαινός , ὅ τ ἐξόπιν ἀργᾶς , [*]
sections.translation: one black , one white of tail , near the palace , on the spear-hand [*] , in a conspicuous place , devouring a hare with offspring unborn

sections.label: Section 1.120
sections.translation:
caught in the last effort to escape . [*]
Sing the song of woe , the song of woe , but may the good prevail !

sections.label: Section 1.122
sections.original: κεδνὸς δὲ στρατόμαντις ἰδὼν δύο λήμασιν ἴσους [*]
sections.translation: Then the wise seer of the host , noticing how the two warlike sons of Atreus were two in temper , recognized the devourers of the hare as the leaders of the army , and

sections.label: Section 1.125
sections.translation: thus interpreted the portent and spoke : In time those who here issue forth shall seize Priam ’ s town , and fate shall violently ravage before its towered walls all the public store of cattle .

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.translation: Only may no jealous god-sent wrath cast its shadow upon the embattled host , the mighty bit forged for Troy ’ s mouth , and strike it before it reaches its goal !

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.translation:
For , in her pity , holy Artemis is angry at the winged hounds of her father , for they sacrifice a wretched timorous thing , together with her young , before she has brought them forth . An abomination to her is the eagles ’ feast .
Sing the song of woe , the song of woe , but may the good prevail !

sections.label: Section 140
sections.translation: Although , O Lovely One , you are so gracious to the tender whelps of fierce lions , and take delight in the suckling young of every wild creature that roams the field , promise that the issue be brought to pass in accordance with these signs , portents

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: δεξιὰ μέν , κατάμομφα δὲ φάσματα [ στρουθῶν ]. [*]
sections.translation: auspicious yet filled with ill . And I implore Paean [*] , the healer , that she may not raise adverse gales with long delay to stay the Danaan fleet from putting forth ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.translation: by urging another sacrifice , one that knows no law , unsuited for feast , worker of family strife , dissolving wife ’ s reverence for husband . For there abides wrath —

sections.label: Section 155
sections.translation:
terrible , not to be suppressed , a treacherous guardian of the home , a wrath that never forgets and that exacts vengeance for a child .
Such utterances of doom , derived from auguries on the march , together with many blessings , did Calchas proclaim to the royal house ; and in harmony with this ,
Sing the song of woe , the song of woe , but may the good prevail !

sections.label: Section 2.160
sections.original: Ζεύς , ὅστις ποτ ἐστίν , εἰ τόδ αὐ-
sections.translation: Zeus , whoever he may be ,— if by this name it pleases him to be invoked , by this name I call to him — as I weigh all things in the balance , I have nothing to compare

sections.label: Section 2.165
sections.translation: save Zeus , if in truth I must cast aside this vain burden from my heart .

sections.label: Section 2.167
sections.translation: He [*] who once was mighty , swelling with insolence for every fight ,

sections.label: Section 2.170
sections.translation: he shall not even be named as having ever existed ; and he [*] who arose later , he has met his overthrower and is past and gone . But whoever willingly sings a victory song for Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 2.175
sections.translation: he shall gain wisdom altogether ,—

sections.label: Section 3.176
sections.translation: Zeus , who sets mortals on the path to understanding , Zeus , who has established as a fixed law that wisdom comes by suffering . But even as trouble , bringing memory of pain , drips over the mind in sleep ,

sections.label: Section 3.180
sections.translation: so wisdom comes to men , whether they want it or not . Harsh , it seems to me , is the grace of gods enthroned upon their awful seats .

sections.label: Section 3.184
sections.translation: So then the captain of the Achaean ships , the elder of the two —

sections.label: Section 3.185
sections.translation: holding no seer at fault , bending to the adverse blasts of fortune , when the Achaean folk , on the shore over against Chalcis

sections.label: Section 3.190
sections.translation: in the region where Aulis ’ tides surge to and fro , were very distressed by opposing winds and failing stores .

sections.label: Section 4.192
sections.translation: The breezes that blew from the Strymon , bringing harmful leisure , hunger , and tribulation of spirit in a cruel port , idle wandering of men , and sparing neither ship

sections.label: Section 4.195
sections.translation: nor cable , began , by doubling the season of their stay , to rub away and wither the flower of Argos ; and when the seer , pointing to Artemis as cause , proclaimed to the chieftains another remedy ,

sections.label: Section 4.200
sections.translation: more oppressive even than the bitter storm , so that the sons of Atreus struck the ground with their canes and did not stifle their tears —

sections.label: Section 4.205
sections.original: ἄναξ δ ὁ πρέσβυς τότ [*] εἶπε φωνῶν :
sections.translation: Then the elder king spoke and said : It is a hard fate to refuse obedience , and hard , if I must slay my child , the glory of my home , and at the altar-side stain

sections.label: Section 4.210
sections.translation: a father ’ s hand with streams of virgin ’ s blood . Which of these courses is not filled with evil ? How can I become a deserter to my fleet and fail my allies in arms ?

sections.label: Section 4.215
sections.original: γᾷ περιόργῳ σφ [*] ἐπιθυμεῖν θέμις . εὖ γὰρ εἴη .”
sections.translation: For that they should with all too impassioned passion crave a sacrifice to lull the winds — even a virgin ’ s blood — stands within their right . May all be for the best .

sections.label: Section 5.220
sections.translation: impious , unholy , unsanctified , from that moment he changed his intention and began to conceive that deed of uttermost audacity . For wretched delusion , counsellor of ill , primal source of woe , makes mortals bold . So then he hardened his heart to sacrifice his daughter

sections.label: Section 5.225
sections.translation: so that he might further a war waged to avenge a woman , and as an offering for the voyage of a fleet !

sections.label: Section 5.228
sections.translation: For her supplications , her cries of Father , and her virgin life ,

sections.label: Section 5.230
sections.translation: the commanders in their eagerness for war cared nothing . Her father , after a prayer , bade his ministers lay hold of her as , enwrapped in her robes , she lay fallen forward ,

sections.label: Section 5.235
sections.translation: and with stout heart to raise her , as if she were a young goat , high above the altar ; and with a gag upon her lovely mouth to hold back the shouted curse against her house —

sections.label: Section 6.238
sections.translation: by the bit ’ s strong and stifling might . Then , as she shed to earth her saffron robe , she

sections.label: Section 6.240
sections.translation: struck each of her sacrificers with a glance from her eyes beseeching pity , looking as if in a picture , wishing she could speak ; for she had often sung where men met at her father ’ s hospitable table ,

sections.label: Section 6.245
sections.original: ἔμελψεν , ἁγνᾷ δ ἀταύρωτος αὐδᾷ [*] πατρὸς
sections.translation: and with her virgin voice would lovingly honor her dear father ’ s prayer for blessing at the third libation [*] —

sections.label: Section 6.248
sections.original: τὰ δ ἔνθεν οὔτ εἶδον οὔτ ἐννέπω :
sections.translation: What happened next I did not see and do not tell . The art of Calchas was not unfulfilled .
